artifact_basis: Stage5E full authority export and sidecar verified earlier
Live root contains OS bundle delivery invariant and validator
Runtime starter smoke from live root returned ALLOW during Stage6A boot probe
fresh_see_basis: Microsoft official Agent Governance Toolkit blog, April 2026
OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026 official page
LangGraph official docs on durable execution, persistence, and HITL
OpenAI Agents SDK official docs on tracing and guardrails
EU AI Act official implementation timeline and FAQ

metablooms_leads: artifact export integrity and user-facing OS bundle delivery guard
failure learning as policy generation and forbidden-method routing
external_peers_lead: per-action tool interception and capability sandboxing
formal mechanical HITL approvals and signed approval tokens
production observability integrations such as LangSmith/OpenTelemetry/Agents SDK tracing
broader OWASP Agentic AI Top 10 coverage

gap_closure_backlog_best_to_worst_priori.why_first: Largest safety surface gap versus Microsoft AGT; converts stage-boundary governance into per-tool-call governance.
deliverables: fixture tests for shell, node, zip, filesystem, web/SEE, and export actions
success_gates: every OS runner that invokes external tools must pass through the wrapper
direct unwrapped tool execution is logged as governance breach in OS receipts
known forbidden methods such as broad unzip -tqq are denied or rerouted

gap_closure_backlog_best_to_worst_priori.why_first: Closes the social-HITL gap; prevents long repairs/export/pointer promotion from proceeding without explicit bounded approval.
success_gates: broad export cannot begin without an approval token
pointer promotion cannot begin without an approval token
the runaway-turn breaker forces audit-only fallback when approval is absent

gap_closure_backlog_best_to_worst_priori.why_first: Turns external security taxonomy into an explicit OS compliance/evidence matrix.
success_gates: all 10 OWASP risks have an owner invariant or explicit gap ticket
coverage matrix is checked during boot and export

gap_closure_backlog_best_to_worst_priori.why_first: Closes LangGraph-style durable execution gap for ChatGPT turn interruptions and long-running OS workflows.
success_gates: interrupted stage resumes without redoing completed side effects
checkpoint history records input, output, hash, and idempotence status

gap_closure_backlog_best_to_worst_priori.why_first: Improves trace usability and ecosystem interoperability after safety-critical gates exist.
success_gates: exported traces validate against OTEL-shaped schema

gap_closure_backlog_best_to_worst_priori.why_first: Small but important internal coherence gap found in live root: registry path exists but ACTIVE_INVARIANTS_INDEX_v1.json was not present at the checked kernel registry location.
deliverables: canonical active invariant index location resolver
boot/export validator requires index presence and membership
success_gates: active index exists at canonical runtime and kernel paths or path map explicitly resolves them
delivery invariant listed in active index

implementation_rule: Implement exactly one stage per turn. Each implementation stage must boot, verify Stage5E or later authority, run SEE if the design has external dependencies, make minimal changes, test fixtures, write receipt/handoff, and export a full OS authority only when the OS bundle delivery guard passes.
